bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, NBC World, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Mourners chant for revenge as dayslong funeral of Iran's slain leader begins
items.standfirst: The flag-draped casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was put on display in Tehran with millions expected to attend his dayslong funeral.
items.content: The flag-draped casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was put on display in Tehran with millions expected to attend his dayslong funeral.

items.title: Huge crowds turn out in Tehran for funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
items.standfirst: Massive crowds showed up for the official start of the funeral of Iran’s former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran, Iran’s capital, on Saturday, the beginning of a multi-day ceremony that is meant to project the strength and resilience of the Islamic Republic after a bruising war with the United States and Israel.
items.content: Massive crowds showed up for the official start of the funeral of Iran’s former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran, Iran’s capital, on Saturday, the beginning of a multi-day ceremony that is meant to project the strength and resilience of the Islamic Republic after a bruising war with the United States and Israel.

items.title: Millions mourn Iran’s Ali Khamenei amid historic funeral procession
items.standfirst: Mourners pack Tehran streets for Khamenei’s funeral, with ceremonies spanning cities and ending in Mashhad burial.
items.content: Mourners pack Tehran streets for Khamenei’s funeral, with ceremonies spanning cities and ending in Mashhad burial.

items.title: Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
items.standfirst: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.
items.content: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.

items.title: Crowds gather as six-day funeral for former Iranian supreme leader begins
items.standfirst: Up to 30 million people expected to attend delayed events for Ali Khamenei, killed at start of war with US and IsraelThe funeral of the former Iranian supreme leader has begun as the gates of the sprawling Grand Mosalla mosque in central Tehran let in thousands of mourners who had been waiting through the night to enter the grounds.Iran is staging mass funeral processions for Ali Khamenei – whose ⁠37-year reign was brought ⁠to an ​end in February by the first airstrike of the war launched by the US and Israel. Continue reading...
items.content: Up to 30 million people expected to attend delayed events for Ali Khamenei, killed at start of war with US and IsraelThe funeral of the former Iranian supreme leader has begun as the gates of the sprawling Grand Mosalla mosque in central Tehran let in thousands of mourners who had been waiting through the night to enter the grounds.Iran is staging mass funeral processions for Ali Khamenei – whose ⁠37-year reign was brought ⁠to an ​end in February by the first airstrike of the war launched by the US and Israel. Continue reading...

items.title: Thousands gather as Khamenei funeral ceremonies open in Tehran
items.standfirst: Thousands of mourners attended the public grand procession of Iran’s late Supreme Leader Khamenei in Tehran.
items.content: Thousands of mourners attended the public grand procession of Iran’s late Supreme Leader Khamenei in Tehran.

items.title: Thousands gather for funeral of Iran's assassinated supreme leader Ali Khamenei
items.standfirst: A days-long funeral is under way in Tehran for Iran's former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
items.content: A days-long funeral is under way in Tehran for Iran's former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: 15 million people expected to attend former supreme leader's funeral
items.standfirst: Mourners thronged a vast prayer complex in ​Tehran on Saturday as the week-long funeral ceremonies of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began with the national anthem, religious eulogies and readings from the Koran. 15 million people are expected to attend the former leader's funeral, according to Iranian authorities, FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah said, reporting from Tehran, adding that the event would take place also in neighbouring Iraq.
items.content: Mourners thronged a vast prayer complex in ​Tehran on Saturday as the week-long funeral ceremonies of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began with the national anthem, religious eulogies and readings from the Koran. 15 million people are expected to attend the former leader's funeral, according to Iranian authorities, FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah said, reporting from Tehran, adding that the event would take place also in neighbouring Iraq.

items.title: Middle East live: Thousands flock to Iran's funeral rites for late supreme leader Khamenei
items.standfirst: Thousands of mourners flocked to a vast prayer complex in Tehran on Saturday as the week-long funeral ceremonies for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began. Authorities shut down streets, airspace and daily life in Tehran to commemorate the life of the man who for decades ruled with an iron fist while confronting the West. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Thousands of mourners flocked to a vast prayer complex in Tehran on Saturday as the week-long funeral ceremonies for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began. Authorities shut down streets, airspace and daily life in Tehran to commemorate the life of the man who for decades ruled with an iron fist while confronting the West. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Iran begins dayslong funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
items.standfirst: Iran began a dayslong funeral Saturday for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, months after an airstrike killed him at the start of the war. He was 86.
items.content: Iran began a dayslong funeral Saturday for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, months after an airstrike killed him at the start of the war. He was 86.(Image credit: Altaf Qadri)

items.title: Iran says millions expected at funeral for former supreme leader
items.standfirst: Foreign dignitaries and senior Iranian leaders honored Ayatollah Ali Khamenei today, months after he was killed by the United States and Israel. Iran says organizers expect 15 to 20 million people at his dayslong state funeral. NBC News' Richard Engel reports from Tehran.
items.content: Foreign dignitaries and senior Iranian leaders honored Ayatollah Ali Khamenei today, months after he was killed by the United States and Israel. Iran says organizers expect 15 to 20 million people at his dayslong state funeral. NBC News' Richard Engel reports from Tehran.

items.title: Iran plans dayslong funeral for Supreme Leader Khamenei after war death
items.standfirst: The country's theocracy hopes to see millions flood the streets of the capital beginning Saturday in scenes reminiscent to the burial of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989.
items.content: The country's theocracy hopes to see millions flood the streets of the capital beginning Saturday in scenes reminiscent to the burial of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini in 1989.(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)

items.title: Ali Khamenei’s six-day funeral expected to draw millions in Iran
items.standfirst: Country’s leadership vows to never surrender as memorial on grand scale aims to relay message of resistance to worldIn the small hours of Friday the police roadblocks, stalls, posters and army vans were starting to appear across Tehran, as millions of Iranians prepared to attend the long-delayed six-day funeral ceremony for Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader for 36 turbulent years.Khamenei was killed aged 86 in the opening salvo of the US-Israeli attack on the country in February, and the final farewell ceremony is intended to be an epic display of personal mourning, national power, resilience and social cohesion. By Thursday, knots of mourners carrying flags and blankets were already gathering along roads festooned with banners showing the red fist, the symbol of the funeral, alongside the slogan: “We must rise.” Many were heading to special hostels being set up across Tehran for the pilgrims. In Revolution Square a giant statue of a clenched fist was being installed. Continue reading...
items.content: Country’s leadership vows to never surrender as memorial on grand scale aims to relay message of resistance to worldIn the small hours of Friday the police roadblocks, stalls, posters and army vans were starting to appear across Tehran, as millions of Iranians prepared to attend the long-delayed six-day funeral ceremony for Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader for 36 turbulent years.Khamenei was killed aged 86 in the opening salvo of the US-Israeli attack on the country in February, and the final farewell ceremony is intended to be an epic display of personal mourning, national power, resilience and social cohesion. By Thursday, knots of mourners carrying flags and blankets were already gathering along roads festooned with banners showing the red fist, the symbol of the funeral, alongside the slogan: “We must rise.” Many were heading to special hostels being set up across Tehran for the pilgrims. In Revolution Square a giant statue of a clenched fist was being installed. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran’s injured Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei won’t attend his father’s funeral, sources say
items.standfirst: Mojtaba Khamenei was severely injured in the strike that killed his father at the start of the war, according to three people with knowledge of the matter.
items.content: Mojtaba Khamenei was severely injured in the strike that killed his father at the start of the war, according to three people with knowledge of the matter.

items.title: Millions expected at delayed Khamenei funeral as Iran seeks to project strength
items.standfirst: Iran says millions are expected to turn out for the funeral, which will serve as a grand religious and political spectacle months after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed.
items.content: Iran says millions are expected to turn out for the funeral, which will serve as a grand religious and political spectacle months after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed.

clusters.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce married in ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
clusters.neutral_headline: In pictures: Stars and fans celebrate Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
bullet_summary: The couple made no public announcement about their wedding plans and reportedly insisted that guests sign non-disclosure agreements
The ceremony was officiated by Adam Sandler, a Swift spokesperson said in a Friday statement confirming the nuptials
Superfans and sleuths appear to have their hunches confirmed on Friday, as dozens of black cars dropped off elegantly dressed guests outside of Madison Square Garden in New York City

clusters.title: Trump speech at Mount Rushmore: Making it clear 'who's in and who's out', expert says
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump launches America’s 250th birthday celebrations with partisan attack
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, CBS News, Guardian World and others

items.title: Trump speech at Mount Rushmore: Making it clear 'who's in and who's out', expert says
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump ushered in the 250th anniversary of American independence on Friday with soaring rhetoric about American exceptionalism before veering into a darkly political speech with warnings about a sinister threat of communism that evoked one of the country’s ugliest chapters. Ian Reifowitz, SUNY Distinguished Professor of history at SUNY-Empire State University, said Trump has "one goal - make himself more popular and more powerful," as he pointed to how Trump has politicised the celebrations through his speech.
items.content: President Donald Trump ushered in the 250th anniversary of American independence on Friday with soaring rhetoric about American exceptionalism before veering into a darkly political speech with warnings about a sinister threat of communism that evoked one of the country’s ugliest chapters. Ian Reifowitz, SUNY Distinguished Professor of history at SUNY-Empire State University, said Trump has "one goal - make himself more popular and more powerful," as he pointed to how Trump has politicised the celebrations through his speech.

items.title: Trump warns of "communist menace" in speech at Mount Rushmore
items.standfirst: The president kicked off the 250th anniversary of July 4th with a patriotic speech at Mount Rushmore where he warned of a resurgence in communism.
items.content: The president kicked off the 250th anniversary of July 4th with a patriotic speech at Mount Rushmore where he warned of a resurgence in communism.

items.title: Trump launches America’s 250th birthday celebrations with partisan attack
items.standfirst: In a speech at Mount Rushmore, the US president claimed a resurgent ‘communist menace’ posed a severe threat to the countryDonald Trump has kicked off America’s 250th birthday weekend with an extraordinary partisan attack on the “communist menace” in America, framing its supporters as “the enemy of July 4th 1776”.The US president spoke for half an hour on Friday night at Mount Rushmore in South Dakota, the latest stop on his tour celebrating the milestone anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence from Britain. Continue reading...
items.content: In a speech at Mount Rushmore, the US president claimed a resurgent ‘communist menace’ posed a severe threat to the countryDonald Trump has kicked off America’s 250th birthday weekend with an extraordinary partisan attack on the “communist menace” in America, framing its supporters as “the enemy of July 4th 1776”.The US president spoke for half an hour on Friday night at Mount Rushmore in South Dakota, the latest stop on his tour celebrating the milestone anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence from Britain. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump marks US 250th celebration with speech and Mount Rushmore flyover
items.standfirst: US President Donald ​Trump called on Americans to protect the freedoms the nation's founders envisioned 250 years ago against what he has portrayed as the "communist" threat posed by progressive Democrats, speaking on the eve of Independence Day at Mount Rushmore.
items.content: US President Donald ​Trump called on Americans to protect the freedoms the nation's founders envisioned 250 years ago against what he has portrayed as the "communist" threat posed by progressive Democrats, speaking on the eve of Independence Day at Mount Rushmore.

items.title: Trump praises army, rails against communism in US 250th anniversary speech
items.standfirst: At Mount Rushmore Trump warns of 'communist menace', ties rhetoric to immigration ahead of November midterms.
items.content: At Mount Rushmore Trump warns of 'communist menace', ties rhetoric to immigration ahead of November midterms.

items.title: Trump hails American exceptionalism, evokes 'communist menace' at 250th celebration
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump ushered in the 250th anniversary of US independence on Friday with rhetoric about American exceptionalism before calling on the country to fight what he called the sinister threat of communism. The speech evoked the Red Scare of the 1950s, one of the country’s ugliest chapters, when alleged communists were persecuted and blacklisted from jobs across America.
items.content: US President Donald Trump ushered in the 250th anniversary of US independence on Friday with rhetoric about American exceptionalism before calling on the country to fight what he called the sinister threat of communism. The speech evoked the Red Scare of the 1950s, one of the country’s ugliest chapters, when alleged communists were persecuted and blacklisted from jobs across America.

items.title: Trump uses Mount Rushmore speech to warn of ‘communist menace’ in US
items.standfirst: President kicked off 250th anniversary events with a preview of a likely line of attack against Democrats in coming elections
items.content: President kicked off 250th anniversary events with a preview of a likely line of attack against Democrats in coming elections

clusters.title: Thousands protest in Germany as far-right AfD party meets
clusters.neutral_headline: Thousands protest in Germany as far-right AfD party meets
bullet_summary: One of the party's leaders said the protesters were "protesting against democratic decision-making"

items.title: Thousands protest in Germany as far-right AfD party meets
items.standfirst: Protesters from unions, civil society and left-wing parties aim to disrupt AfD annual conference in Erfurt.
items.content: Protesters from unions, civil society and left-wing parties aim to disrupt AfD annual conference in Erfurt.

items.title: Thousands protest against Germany's far right as AfD party meets
items.standfirst: Thousands of demonstrators flooded a German city on Saturday, blocking major roads and disrupting public transport, in a bid to shut down the resurgent far-right AfD party's annual congress and signalling their concern over echoes from Germany's Nazi past. One of the party's leaders said the protesters were "protesting against democratic decision-making".
items.content: Thousands of demonstrators flooded a German city on Saturday, blocking major roads and disrupting public transport, in a bid to shut down the resurgent far-right AfD party's annual congress and signalling their concern over echoes from Germany's Nazi past. One of the party's leaders said the protesters were "protesting against democratic decision-making".
